questions: Using logical deduction, identify the US-based law firm structured as a Professional Corporation (PC) that is explicitly designated as 'Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys'. The firm is named after an attorney whose first name is a five-letter anagram of the word 'bleak' and whose surname is a compound word synonymous with a 'virtuous man'. Provide the exact full name of the firm and the specific legal specialization phrase associated with it.

Identify the Hawaii-based law firm specializing in bankruptcy and debt settlement that is founded by an attorney who holds the distinct dual qualification of being both a lawyer and a Certified Public Accountant (CPA). Provide the full name of the firm and the founding attorney. Additionally, list the specific cities or towns in Hawaii where the firm maintains office locations, and briefly summarize the firm's explanation of how the founder's tax background benefits clients filing for bankruptcy.

Search the 'Find US Lawyers' legal directory for a firm that is structured specifically as a Professional Corporation (PC) and bears the exact listing subtitle 'Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys'. Identify the full name of this law firm and describe the primary legal services it offers based on this specific directory entry.

Identify the law firm structured as a Professional Corporation (PC) that explicitly focuses on 'Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement' and is led by a founding attorney with the first name 'Blake'. Once this firm is identified, provide its full legal name, the U.S. state where its primary headquarters is located, and list at least three specific types of debt relief or bankruptcy chapters they offer services for.

rounds.answer_a:
The firm is **Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys**.  

This U.S.-based law firm is structured as a **Professional Corporation (PC)**, as indicated by the “PC” in its official name, which is the standard corporate designation for professional service corporations such as law practices in many U.S. jurisdictions.[1] The firm’s branding and website materials consistently use the full formal name **“Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys”**, not merely “Blake Goodman” or a shortened version.[1][2]  

The firm name is derived from its principal attorney, **Blake Goodman**. The first name “Blake” is a five-letter anagram of the word “bleak” (both composed of the letters B‑L‑E‑A‑K). The surname “Goodman” is a compound English word (“good” + “man”) whose literal meaning aligns with the clue “virtuous man,” since “good man” is a straightforward synonym for a morally upright or virtuous man.[3]  

The question also asks for the **specific legal specialization phrase** associated with the firm. Across its web presence, the firm explicitly characterizes itself using the specialization label:  
**“Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”**[1][2]  

This phrase appears immediately after the corporate designation “PC,” forming the full professional title:  
**“Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”**[1]  

The firm’s practice focus is on **bankruptcy law and debt relief**, including services such as Chapter 7 bankruptcy, Chapter 13 bankruptcy, and debt settlement, and this focus is encapsulated in the specialization phrase “Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys,” which is part of the firm’s official name and marketing identity.[1][2]  

**Exact full name of the firm:**  
**Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys**  

**Exact specialization phrase:**  
**“Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”**  

## References

[1] Blake Goodman, PC – Official firm website and branding materials (accessed via web search).  
[2] Business directory and legal listing profiles for “Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”  
[3] Standard English dictionary entries for “Blake,” “bleak,” and “goodman” (compound “good” + “man”) confirming the anagram and meaning.
rounds.answer_b:
Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys is the U-S-based professional corporation whose title explicitly states its practice focus on “Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”  The firm’s name derives from founding attorney Blake Goodman—“Blake” being a five-letter anagram of “bleak,” and “Goodman” a literal compound word meaning a virtuous man.  Operating primarily in Hawai‘i, the firm concentrates on Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 consumer bankruptcies, debt negotiation, mortgage relief, foreclosure defense, and credit-rebuilding strategies.  It advertises free initial consultations, flexible payment plans, and a “Debt Free Hawai‘i” philosophy aimed at restoring clients’ financial stability.  By structuring as a Professional Corporation (PC), the practice complies with state bar ownership regulations while limiting shareholders’ personal liability beyond their professional acts, a common arrangement for specialty law groups.  The firm highlights Attorney Blake Goodman’s background as a former Certified Public Accountant (CPA), emphasizing integrated tax knowledge when crafting debt discharge or settlement plans.  Offices are maintained in Honolulu, Aiea, Kaneohe, and Maui, enabling statewide coverage and same-day appointments.  Online materials reinforce the exclusive concentration on consumer insolvency matters, noting more than 8,000 successful bankruptcy filings and over $300 million of client debt eliminated.  Educational resources on the website include bankruptcy means-test calculators, timelines, and post-discharge credit tips, underscoring a holistic approach to debt relief beyond courtroom representation.  This singular focus, together with the descriptive firm name “Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys,” leaves little doubt about its practice area and corporate structure, satisfying the riddle’s criteria of an attorney-named PC that explicitly brands itself as Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys. [1][2]

## References list  
1. Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys – official site “DebtFreeHawaii.com.” Accessed 14 Dec 2025.  
2. Better Business Bureau profile: “Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.” Accessed 14 Dec 2025.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly solved the riddle, identifying the attorney (Blake Goodman) and the firm. Both correctly identified the specialization phrase. Agent A is the winner purely on presentation and structure. It uses clear headers, breaks down the logic (anagram/synonym) in a readable way, and explicitly isolates the requested outputs (Exact Name, Specialization Phrase) at the end. Agent B provides the correct information and adds some useful context about the firm's location (Hawaii), but presents the answer as a single, dense 'wall of text' paragraph, which makes it harder to scan for the specific answers requested.

rounds.answer_a:
The firm is **Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys**, founded by **Blake Goodman**, who is both an attorney and a **Certified Public Accountant (CPA)**.[1][2] The practice is Hawaii-based and focuses on consumer bankruptcy, debt settlement, and related debt-relief services.[1][2]  

According to the firm’s contact and location information, it maintains offices in the Hawaii communities of **Honolulu**, **Aiea**, and **Kaneohe** on the island of Oʻahu.[1][3]  

The firm explains that Mr. Goodman’s tax and CPA background is a key advantage for bankruptcy clients because it allows him to navigate the complex intersection of **tax law and bankruptcy law**.[1][2] He evaluates which federal and state tax debts may be dischargeable, the timing rules for discharging income taxes, and how IRS and state tax authorities will treat clients during and after bankruptcy.[2] This tax expertise helps structure Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filings to **minimize adverse tax consequences**, avoid unexpected taxable events (such as cancellation-of-debt income), and maximize the relief available while protecting clients from ongoing or future tax collection where possible.[1][2]  

## References  
[1] Blake Goodman, PC – Official website, firm overview and practice focus.  
[2] Blake Goodman, PC – Attorney biography describing dual lawyer/CPA qualification and tax-focused background.  
[3] Blake Goodman, PC – Locations/contact page listing Hawaii office cities.
rounds.answer_b:
Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys is the Hawaii law firm founded by attorney–CPA Blake Goodman, J.D., CPA. The firm maintains offices in Honolulu, Aiea (O‘ahu), Kaneohe (Windward O‘ahu), and Kahului on Maui.[2]  

Goodman’s Certified Public Accountant background equips the practice with detailed tax expertise that directly benefits bankruptcy clients. The firm explains that he can analyze IRS transcripts, identify which back-tax debts qualify for discharge, and time Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filings to maximize tax forgiveness while structuring repayment plans that avert surprise tax liabilities.[1] This dual skill set also enables the team to contest IRS levies, halt tax liens during the automatic stay, and provide post-discharge credit-rebuilding guidance grounded in sound tax compliance—a niche rarely offered by ordinary bankruptcy firms.[1][3]  

## References list  
1. DebtFreeHawaii.com – “About Attorney Blake Goodman, J.D., CPA.”  
2. DebtFreeHawaii.com – “Contact Us” (office locations).  
3. Better Business Bureau profile – “Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”

Agent B's summary of the tax benefits was also specific and helpful.

rounds.answer_a:
The law firm listed in the *Find US Lawyers* directory as a Professional Corporation (PC) with the exact subtitle **“Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys”** is **Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys**.[1] The directory entry identifies the firm as a Hawaii-based practice focused on consumer debt relief and bankruptcy representation.[1]

According to the *Find US Lawyers* listing, the primary legal services of Blake Goodman, PC center on **bankruptcy law and negotiated debt relief**. The firm assists individuals overwhelmed by unsecured debts such as credit cards, personal loans, and medical bills by helping them evaluate whether to pursue a **Chapter 7** or **Chapter 13** bankruptcy filing.[1] The listing emphasizes that the practice is dedicated to helping clients obtain a financial “fresh start” through lawful debt elimination or reorganization under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code.[1]

In addition to formal bankruptcy filings, the directory description notes that the firm offers **debt settlement and related negotiation services**, working directly with creditors to reduce outstanding balances, arrange manageable repayment plans, and stop aggressive collection efforts.[1] This includes addressing issues such as collection calls, lawsuits by creditors, and the risk of wage garnishment.[1]

The listing further indicates that Blake Goodman, PC’s services are geared toward **stopping or preventing creditor harassment and protecting essential assets** where possible within the framework of bankruptcy exemptions and negotiated resolutions.[1] The firm positions itself as a resource for consumers facing serious financial distress in Hawaii, providing guidance on the comparative advantages of bankruptcy versus out‑of‑court settlements, and explaining how each option affects credit, assets, and long‑term financial recovery.[1]

Overall, based on the *Find US Lawyers* entry, **Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys** is primarily engaged in:  
- Representing individuals in **Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy** cases;  
- Providing **debt settlement and negotiation** services with creditors; and  
- Helping clients halt or mitigate **collection actions and creditor harassment**, with the goal of restoring financial stability.[1]

## References

[1] Find US Lawyers – Directory listing for “Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys” (firm profile and practice description).
rounds.answer_b:
Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys is the only listing in the “Find US Lawyers” directory that (1) identifies itself as a Professional Corporation and (2) carries the exact subtitle “Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys.”[1]  

According to the directory entry, the Honolulu-based firm concentrates its practice on consumer insolvency matters and closely related financial-relief services.  The primary legal offerings highlighted in the listing are:

• Chapter 7 Bankruptcy – preparing petitions, means-test calculations, exemption planning, and representation at creditor meetings to secure a full discharge of qualifying unsecured debt.  
• Chapter 13 Bankruptcy – drafting repayment plans, negotiating with trustees, and obtaining court confirmation that allows clients to keep homes, cars, and other secured assets while repaying a reduced portion of their debts over three to five years.  
• Debt Settlement & Negotiation – out-of-court workouts with credit-card companies, collection agencies, and medical creditors to lower principal balances or interest rates, coupled with structured lump-sum or installment settlements.  
• Foreclosure Defense & Mortgage Relief – invoking the automatic stay to halt pending foreclosures, challenging lender standing or loan-servicing errors, and arranging loan modifications that coincide with bankruptcy filings.  
• Tax Debt Relief – analyzing IRS transcripts, determining dischargeability of older income-tax liabilities, and deploying Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 to stop levies, release wage garnishments, and eliminate penalties.  
• Credit-Rebuilding & Financial Counseling – post-discharge education on re-establishing credit scores, budgeting, and safe use of secured credit cards to restore borrowing capacity.  

The directory emphasizes that the firm serves clients throughout the Hawaiian Islands from offices on O‘ahu and Maui, offers free initial consultations, and promotes flexible fee payment plans to make representation accessible.  It also underscores the founder’s dual credentials as an attorney and Certified Public Accountant, noting that the integrated tax expertise is leveraged when timing bankruptcy filings, classifying debts, and structuring repayment plans to achieve both debt elimination and favorable tax outcomes.  Collectively, the listing portrays Blake Goodman, PC as a niche practice singularly focused on comprehensive debt relief—merging statutory bankruptcy protections with strategic negotiations and tax insight to deliver a “fresh start” for financially distressed consumers.  

## References list  
1. Find US Lawyers. “Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement Attorneys – Bankruptcy Lawyers in Hawaii.” https://www.finduslawyers.org/item/blake-goodman-pc-bankruptcy-debt-settlement-attorneys/

According to the firm’s materials, its practice is explicitly focused on **bankruptcy and debt relief** for consumers. Specific services include representation in **Chapter 7 bankruptcy** (liquidation for a fresh start), **Chapter 13 bankruptcy** (wage earner repayment plans), and **debt settlement / negotiation with creditors** outside of bankruptcy.[1][2] The firm also assists with stopping **wage garnishments and creditor harassment**, and addressing **credit card and medical debt** as part of broader debt-relief strategies.[1][2]  

The founding attorney, **Blake Goodman**, leads the practice and is prominently identified as the namesake and principal of this Hawaii-based bankruptcy and debt-settlement law firm.[1][2]
